programme.title: The Zoo: San Diego
programme.desc: Mek the tiger returns to the zoo after six years. Two shy tree kangaroos are introduced to one another and the keepers have to move more than 100 animals

programme.title: The Zoo: San Diego
programme.desc: The keepers wave goodbye to two elephant brothers as they start their new adventure at a zoo in Texas. Plus, two African penguin chicks join the colony

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: Warden Marshall leads a poaching investigation, Richards visits an illegal snake breeder and Gould and Hickerson try to save an injured pelican

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Rocky, a young pitbull, fights for his life after being bitten by a rattlesnake, while Sassquatch the cat suffers after a fire at his owners apartment

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson and the gang look back at builds from the past, and gather around the campfire to tell funny stories and laugh about the jokes they have played on each other

programme.title: My Extreme Animal Phobia
programme.desc: Phobia expert Dr Robin Zasio comes to the aid of Katie, who is afraid of spiders and will go out of her way to secure every nook and cranny against them. The programme also features Andrews fear of cockroaches and Jackies fear of butterflies

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: Atz Lees return to the Hunters Cabin is rudely interrupted, and he turns to a veteran homesteader for some much-needed help. Plus, the Kilchers join forces to protect Shanes cabin

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.sub-title: The Old Man on the Mountain
programme.desc: Neil prepares to reopen the Motherlode mine, but a new arrival could bring an end to his mining empire. Meanwhile, Jeremy starts a new venture, and two rivals form an unlikely bond

programme.title: Deadly Islands
programme.desc: Dave Salmoni reveals the hardship and dangers - not to mention the broken equipment - he and his team had to contend with during the filming of the series

programme.title: Catfishin Kings
programme.desc: The teams brave treacherous lake conditions to show their Texas noodlin

programme.title: Speed of Life
programme.desc: Super-slow motion images reveal the intricacies of daily life in the natural world. The first programme captures a Harris hawks attack on a partridge

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson finds the perfect treehouse spot for a couple seeking meditation space. The team creates an energy-infused retreat 20ft up in a twisting pair of maples

programme.title: Animal Cops Philadelphia
programme.desc: A man is suspected of violence toward three cats, and a dog whose vision was being restricted by its matted fur receives help

programme.title: Animal Cops Philadelphia
programme.desc: A dead horse is found at a farm in northern Pennsylvania, where 13 other horses are living in dreadful conditions. Plus, an emaciated dog and a cat with a large tumour are treated. Documentary following the work of the SPCAs Humane Law Enforcement team, which investigates cases of animal cruelty

programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy comes to the aid of George and Beth, who are virtual prisoners in their own home due to their vicious kitty Reese

programme.title: Must Love Cats
programme.desc: John Fulton explores Japans fascination with the animals by visiting cat cafes, and meets animal lovers who risk their lives to save pets left homeless by natural disasters

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: Wardens question a felon suspected of illegally hunting with a firearm. Meanwhile, a late-night shrimp boat search uncovers illegal fish

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff and Dr Petra work together to save a beloved pet huskys leg. Specialist Dr Baier has a scare during the routine treatment of a pet pig

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff and the team are on a time limit to treat an English bulldog who swallowed a rock. Hector gets a workout from some puppies in need

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Tia races to rescue four dogs at a rural shelter in Louisiana from euthanasia. Mariah and Toney give a scared, shy dog a shot at a forever home

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: The team are faced with an emotional and physical challenge in the form of a severely under-nourished pit bull. They also have to rehabilitate a dog left in a shop

programme.title: The Zoo: San Diego
programme.desc: The youngest elephant at the park learns how to use its trunk, while an Andean bear is ready to start mating. Jeff searches for the elusive coast patch-nosed snake

programme.title: The Zoo: San Diego
programme.desc: A white rhino is pregnant with a very special calf. Plus, Connor the bone-loving Malayan tiger has tooth trouble and the vets step in to help

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson creates an off-the-grid escape that is all about peace, love, and treehouses. Once built, the owner adds a massive hammock in the trees

programme.title: Preposterous Pets
programme.desc: The story of an obsessive fan of lizards, who changed his name to Henry Lizardlover and found fame by taking photos of his scaly friends posing as humans

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: How does a fish make a weapon out of water? Why do turtles always return to the same beach to lay their eggs? Why do rhinos all poop in the same place?

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Experts spill the secrets of the means of communication used by cats. Plus, some clever monkeys who have turned to a life of crime

programme.title: Lair Of The Sawfish
programme.desc: Experts unravel the mystery behind one of the oceans strangest creatures, the sawfish, from its evolution to its role in the marine ecosystem

programme.title: Alaska: Surviving the Last Frontier
programme.desc: Following four groups of ordinary people as they travel into the wilderness of Alaska, where they must find a new home in the inhospitable environment and deal with the threat posed by bears, glaciers and their own fears

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Brett Raymer and Wayde King revisit some of the most unthinkable items they have turned into aquariums over the years, and share never-before-seen footage of their making

programme.title: Tanked
programme.sub-title: Betty Whites Got an App for That
programme.desc: Brett Raymer and Wayde King undertake a job for veteran actress Betty White, involving building an aquarium with a Noahs Ark theme. They also work on a smartphone-inspired tank

programme.title: Fish or Die
programme.desc: The arapaima is the worlds largest scaled freshwater fish and considered the ultimate jungle catch. The crew heads to Ecuador to land one of these living dinosaurs

programme.title: Catfishin Kings
programme.desc: Following expert hand-fishers as they compete in a cross-state catfish-grabbing tournament, hosted by Brady Knowlton

programme.title: Expedition Bigfoot
programme.desc: With just three days left in the Kentucky expedition, the team investigates a strange tree structure that could be key to proving the existence of Bigfoot

programme.title: Lair Of The Sawfish
programme.desc: Experts unravel the mystery behind one of the oceans strangest creatures, the sawfish, from its evolution to its role in the marine ecosystem

programme.title: My Extreme Animal Phobia
programme.desc: Tom tries to conquer his fear of rodents, Cameron battles his hatred of birds, Jackie addresses her dog phobia and Ramona deals with her terror around snakes

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Roy Sings Orbison
programme.desc: The singer performs some of his greatest hits, including Only the Lonely, Crying, Penny Arcade, Blue Bayou, Running Scared, Candy Man, In Dreams, Mean Woman Blues, Its Over, Oh and Pretty Woman

programme.title: Scotland: Romes Final Frontier
programme.desc: Archaeologist Dr Fraser Hunter reflects on three centuries of interaction and conflict between the Romans and ancient Scottish tribes. He examines whether the Scots were too strong for the invading army or if the country was simply considered not worth the effort of conquering. He also focuses on the first Tay Bridge, the first depiction of tartan and forgotten Roman camps that once held 35,000 men

programme.title: Shark
programme.desc: A look at how scientists are challenging traditional perceptions of sharks, with breakthroughs regarding the creatures intelligence and social skills presenting them in a surprising new light. The programme also highlights some researchers beliefs that studying the predators could help to solve some of the most pressing medical challenges of the 21st century. Narrated by Paul McGann

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents live coverage of the evening session on day seven at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the of conclusion of a second-round match and the second session of another. Chris Wakelin was the first player to progress to the quarter-finals last year, overcoming Mark Allen 13-6, despite Allens maximum 147 break in the second session. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Ken Doherty, Steven Hallworth, Stephen Hendry, John Parrott, Joe Perry, Dennis Taylor and Lee Walker

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gary Davies presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on April 23, 1987. Featuring Five Star, The Smiths, Kim Wilde and Junior, Judy Boucher, Starship, Tom Jones, Glenn and Chris, Madonna and Terence Trent DArby

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles hosts the edition first aired on April 19, with performances by artists including Sonia, the Quireboys, Adamski, the Blues Brothers, Alannah Myles, the Adventures of Stevie V, Faith No More, the Family Stand and Madonna

programme.title: Roy Orbison at the BBC
programme.desc: A selection of archive performances for a celebration of the singer-songwriter, who Elvis Presley famously described as the best singer in the world. Featuring hits Only the Lonely, Crying and Oh, Pretty Woman, In Dreams and I Drove All Night

programme.title: Roy Orbison and Friends: A Black and White Night (1988)
programme.desc: In a programme first broadcast in 1988 - the year of his death - the singer performs his greatest hits, including Oh, Pretty Woman, Its Over and Crying. He is joined on stage by stars including Bruce Springsteen, Tom Waits, Elvis Costello, Jackson Browne, kd lang, Jennifer Warnes and Bonnie Raitt. They are backed by the TCB Band, who played with Elvis Presley in his latter years, and whose members include James Burton, Glen D Hardin, Jerry Scheff and Ronnie Tutt. Filmed at the Cocoanut Grove nightclub in Los Angeles

programme.title: Timewatch
programme.desc: A profile of the 20th-century escapologist, featuring restored archive footage and interviews with historians and illusionists, who recall his impact on audiences. Plus, revealing some of the secrets behind some of Houdinis most famous escapes. Originally broadcast in 2000

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gary Davies presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on April 23, 1987. Featuring Five Star, The Smiths, Kim Wilde and Junior, Judy Boucher, Starship, Tom Jones, Glenn and Chris, Madonna and Terence Trent DArby

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles hosts the edition first aired on April 19, with performances by artists including Sonia, the Quireboys, Adamski, the Blues Brothers, Alannah Myles, the Adventures of Stevie V, Faith No More, the Family Stand and Madonna

programme.title: Roy Orbison at the BBC
programme.desc: A selection of archive performances for a celebration of the singer-songwriter, who Elvis Presley famously described as the best singer in the world. Featuring hits Only the Lonely, Crying and Oh, Pretty Woman, In Dreams and I Drove All Night

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents live coverage of the evening session on day eight at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the of conclusion of a second-round match and the opening session of another. Chinese stars Ding Junhui and Zhao Xintong could be involved in the match drawing to a close should they have won their respective first-round contests. After years of Ding being in contention to be the first player from his country to win this title, the honour fell to Zhao last year and he defeated another of his compatriots in Lei Peifan at this stage of the competition. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Ken Doherty, Steven Hallworth, Stephen Hendry, John Parrott, Joe Perry, Dennis Taylor and Lee Walker

programme.title: Alex Higgins: The Peoples Champion
programme.desc: James Nesbitt narrates a look back at the often controversial life of the two-time world snooker champion, who was widely regarded as one of the most naturally gifted players in the history of his sport, but whose brilliance was flawed by his demons. With contributions by members of his family, as well as fellow snooker greats Jimmy White, Ronnie OSullivan, Steve Davis and Dennis Taylor

programme.title: Gold Run (2022)
programme.desc: Wartime drama telling the true story of how 55 tons of gold was transported through rough winter landscapes on trucks, trains and boats? by a group of unlikely resistance fighters ahead of the invading Nazi forces.? Starring Jon Oigarden, Thorbjorn Harr and Anatole Taubman. In Norwegian

programme.title: Gershwins Summertime: The Song That Conquered the World
programme.desc: How George Gershwin and DuBose Heywards song Summertime became a standard around the world. The film tells the story of its first appearance in the 1935 opera Porgy and Bess, charts how it has been reinterpreted by artists including Billie Holiday, Janis Joplin, Mahalia Jackson, Miles Davis and Ella Fitzgerald, and explores the many meanings that have been derived from it through the decades. Narrated by Pauline Black

programme.title: Why the Industrial Revolution Happened Here
programme.desc: Jeremy Black examines one of the most extraordinary periods in English history, explaining the unique economic, social and political conditions that led to the nation becoming the richest and most powerful on Earth by the 19th century. He traces the unprecedented explosion of new ideas and technological inventions that transformed an agrarian society into an increasingly industrial and urbanised one

programme.title: Alex Higgins: The Peoples Champion
programme.desc: James Nesbitt narrates a look back at the often controversial life of the two-time world snooker champion, who was widely regarded as one of the most naturally gifted players in the history of his sport, but whose brilliance was flawed by his demons. With contributions by members of his family, as well as fellow snooker greats Jimmy White, Ronnie OSullivan, Steve Davis and Dennis Taylor

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Gaby Roslin presents the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.sub-title: Murderers, Messages and the Breakfast Killer
programme.desc: In a case of mistaken identity, an intelligence analyst helps convict the gang that stabbed a man to death and a trace evidence expert finds the weapon used to kill south Wales teenager Rebecca Aylward

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts uncovers some 1960s newspapers in the Hampshire town of Gosport, Martel Maxwell is impressed with whats on offer in Driffield in the East Riding of Yorkshire, and Tommy Walsh feels at home at a one-bedroom flat in Hackney, east London

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian and the team are in Newark, where they hear the story of how a young local goalkeeper took part in the 1914 Christmas Day football match between British and German troops - and may even have provided the ball that was used

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Brenda finds love at the home of a clock collector, but it may just be that she has fallen for a killer. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams and Ruby-May Martinwood

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Briony May Williams assists a couple from London in the market for a new home in the countryside of Oxfordshire or Wiltshire that has enough space for them and their two young sons. Taking time away from house hunting, Briony learns why the relatively new practice of not mowing the grass in the month of May is here to stay

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Now retired, ex-police officer Neil wants to build a successful woodworking business, So far however, the orders havent been coming in. Mentor Bruce thinks hes doing too much and needs to create a core range to have a chance at success. When her father died, Catherine decided to make her embroidery business dreams a reality, but hasnt been able to earn a living from it. Her mentor Stephanie thinks she has great potential, but is spreading herself too thin and needs to streamline her work. Presented by Dominic Chinea

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which Justin, Dean, Serena, Joe and Andrea race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Angela Scanlon for the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Angellica Bell and Lauren Laverne present the magazine-style programme with usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits
programme.sub-title: Pregnant and Jailed in Post Office Scandal
programme.desc: On her first day running her local Post Office, Seema discovered a cash shortfall at the till. She had to put in her own money to make the books add up. Every day, more and more money appeared to be missing. She didnt know it then, but she was the latest victim of the infamous Post Office Horizon accounting scandal - and eventually she was convicted and sent to prison, while pregnant. Here, Bill pairs her with artist Jack, whose detailed paintings explore resilience, as she tells her remarkable story

programme.title: Beyond Paradise
programme.desc: Humphrey and Esther are left baffled when a dogwalker leads them to a man painted green and bound with ivy. Delving into the mystery of the mythical Green Man, the team discover an off-grid community living off the land. But when their woodland home comes under threat from a developer from the city, tensions rise and accusations fly as the image of the Green Man continues to crop up at crime scenes. Cosy crime drama, starring Kris Marshall

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Sue Perkins hosts the satirical current affairs quiz, with comedian Finlay Christie and journalist Camilla Long joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: A couple of visiting girls catch Conor and Jocks attention, so they decide its high time they entered the Cork dating scene. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Whalley

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: Claudia presents highlights from her first series, featuring appearances from Lisa Kudrow, Jennifer Saunders, Niall Horan, Guz Khan, Jeff Goldblum, James McAvoy, Rita Wilson, Jamie Dornan, Anna Faris, Jimmy Carr, Ralph Fiennes, Rachel Zegler and Jamie Dornan

programme.title: Chaos Walking (2021)
programme.desc: A woman is stranded on a planet with an all-male population where a mysterious force makes thoughts and feelings visible. She works together with a local to survive, and unlock the strange worlds dark secrets. Sci-fi adventure based on Patrick Ness novel The Knife of Never Letting Go, starring Tom Holland, Daisy Ridley, Demian Bichir, David Oyelowo and Mads Mikkelsen

programme.title: Candyman (2021)
programme.desc: Artist Anthony McCoy and his art gallery director girlfriend Brianna move into the heavily gentrified Cabrini Green neighbourhood of Chicago, where the urban legend of the Candyman once struck fear into residents hearts. For his provocative new exhibition, Anthony plunders the old legend. Sequel to the 1992 horror movie, starring Yahya Abdul-Mateen II and Vanessa Williams

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Georgina Hayden and Richard Bainbridge, with guest Nish Kumar and the winner of the Great British Menu. Helen McGinn picks the wines

programme.title: Flaming Feasts
programme.desc: Wrexham has burst onto the global stage in recent years thanks to a Hollywood football story that has captured imaginations far beyond Wales. Chris Roberts looks past the headlines to sample the city through the people who grow, brew and cook its food. His tour includes visits to Wrexham Lager Brewery, which was close to disappearing but has been revived by a group of locals, and Bellis Brothers Farm, a fifth-generation family business that began with a single strawberry crop in 1860. He also explores the citys evolving food scene at Harvest Vegetarian Kitchen and takes in the vibrant Portuguese community

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Alex Scott and guests look ahead to the weekends matches, including Manchester City v Southampton and Chelsea v Leeds United in the FA Cup semi-finals. Plus, news from the lunchtime Premier League game between Fulham and Aston Villa

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents the show from Shepton Mallet, Somerset, with the reds and blues led by John Cameron and Stephanie Connell

programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Gabby Logan is joined by former world marathon record-holder Paula Radcliffe to look ahead to Sundays event, profiling contenders for the mens, womens and wheelchair races, and telling the stories of inspirational amateur runners

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents coverage of the afternoon session on day eight at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the opening session of one second-round match and the second session of another. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Ken Doherty, Steven Hallworth, Stephen Hendry, John Parrott, Joe Perry, Dennis Taylor and Lee Walker

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manchester City v Southampton (kick-off 5.15pm). All the action from the opening semi-final at Wembley Stadium. City claimed an impressive 4-0 win over Liverpool in the quarter-finals, with Erling Haaland scoring a hat-trick, and are the favourites to go one better than last season and lift the cup. However, Championship outfit Southampton have already caused a major upset in the last eight, overcoming Arsenal 2-1 and are now looking to reach the final for the first time since 2003

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts the revival of the comedy quiz show, in which Fred Sirieix, Laura Whitmore, Eddie Kadi, Josh Widdicombe, Vanessa Feltz and Scarlett Moffatt fill in the blanks to help contestants win some brilliant prizes

programme.title: The Weakest Link
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan is joined by contestants Aisling Bea, La Voix, Ben Bailey Smith, Anna Richardson, Samantha Kinghorn, Will Best, Jason Byrne and Jacqui Joseph. They must initially work together to answer general knowledge questions and bank as much money as possible for their chosen charities, before voting off round by round

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A mystery virus sweeps across Holby. Flynn is confronted with his old CO from the army, and works with Byron to restore order when an accident causes a blackout. Sarah questions Dylans commitment to fatherhood in light of the secrets he is keeping, Stevie refuses counselling in the aftermath of her recent loss, and Cam struggles to cope on his return to work

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee fails in his bid to buy tickets to see his favourite band Oasis in concert, after briefly leaving his tablet in Lucys possession and somehow losing his place in the queue. A year on, he still cant come to terms with the disappointment. Lucy suggests a night in watching Oasis on the television instead, alongside an Indian takeaway. However, Lee cannot get over a feeling hes had for a long time - that it was entirely Lucys fault that he missed out. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Action from Manchester City v Southampton at Wembley Stadium in the semi-finals of the FA Cup, and Premier League matches, including Arsenal v Newcastle United at Emirates Stadium. The Saints were looking for a second successive major scalp in the competition after overcoming Arsenal in the previous round. Meanwhile, the Gunners continued their title challenge against an inconsistent Newcastle side. Plus, Fulham v Aston Villa, Liverpool v Crystal Palace, West Ham United v Everton and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Tottenham Hotspur

programme.title: Wayne Rooney Meets
programme.desc: The former England striker talks to Manchester United captain Bruno Fernandes, who will represent Portugal at the forthcoming FIFA World Cup

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Sick of dating creeps, Lucinda is given the royal princess treatment courtesy of her fairy godmother, as Sal auditions for a talent show and Dickie offers sage advice in the work place. Comedy, written by and starring Phil Dunning, with Mark Benton, Michelle Visage, Alexandra Mardell, Patsy Lowe and Elijah Young

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Animated sketch show set in Belfast, where the cats get stoned, the pigeons get philosophical and Gambo forgets how to breathe

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Part one of two. The journalist and media personality meets four young women who have reported being raped by someone they know. With unprecedented access to women who have waived their right to anonymity after reporting being raped, Stacey spends three years following the complainants in the lead-up to trial. She explores the challenges of reporting rape when the alleged perpetrator is someone familiar, and asks whether the justice system delivers what those desperate to be believed hope for

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Part two of two. As Stacey continues to follow the emotional journey of the young women who have reported rape and now trying to navigate the justice system, she learns to appreciate that the statistics are stark. Fewer than two per cent of reported sexual assaults result in conviction, though this increases to over 50 per cent for cases that proceed to trial. As their trials near, the women learn that credibility also plays a pivotal role

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May bid to prove that cars can be art by filling a gallery in Middlesbrough with motoring-related works, including a few creations of their own. They also resort to unusual promotional techniques to attract more visitors to their exhibition. Back on the track, Jeremy tests British supercar the Noble M600. Jenson Button drives the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: As a new mother, Staceys worries for the future - and about death - have only exacerbated. In order to try to overcome her fears, she wants to face death head on, so joins the team of funeral directors at Nottinghamshire-based firm, AW Lymn, one of the largest independent funeral enterprises in the country. Stacey fully immerses herself in every aspect of the companys day-to-day work to discover more about the business of death - from arranging funerals to making coffins, learning more about embalming to carving elaborate headstones - and tries to get the answers she has been searching for

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to Borneo, where he meets baby orangutans and encounters a python on a night-time river expedition

programme.title: Despicable Me (2010)
programme.desc: A criminal mastermind hopes to seal his reputation as the greatest villain of them all by stealing the Moon. First he needs to get his hands on a shrinking ray owned by a rival evil genius, and adopts three orphan girls to help him - but finds that fatherhood brings out his long-buried caring side. Animated comedy, with the voices of Steve Carell, Jason Segel, Russell Brand and Julie Andrews

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: While investigating an island-wide blackout, Neville and the team discovers an electrocuted body. Their investigation leads them into a hidden world of data mining, crypto currency and stealing electricity. The only problem is that all three suspects were together at the time of the victims electrocution. So if it was murder, how on earth did they do it?

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: Comic crime drama about an ambitious young detective who has a surprise encounter with the retired actor who played her TV cop show hero while investigating a suicide. He claims that the death was actually a murder, and when forensics prove him right, she invites him to help her crack the case. Starring Timothy Spall and Gwyneth Keyworth

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: After a walker is found dead on a Welsh mountain trail, Johns insight into the unidentified woman sets a murder investigation in motion. Janie ropes a reluctant John into joining her mothers walking group to help solve the case. Janies fierce defence of one of the suspects reveals more about her past, while John finds himself out of the house and enjoying himself a lot more than hes ready to admit. Guest starring Vicki Pepperdine

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Comedy about a gang of friends who are fiercely proud of their north-eastern town of Middlesbrough and their small pocket of the LGBTQ+ community. Dickies friends rally around him when his boyfriend dumps him on the doorstep. He vows to fight for his love and win back his man. Written by and starring Phil Dunning, with Mark Benton, Alexandra Mardell, Patsy Lowe and Elijah Young

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard embarks on a journey to gather together some of Kens nearest and dearest for his surprise 75th birthday party. However, tracking down the elusive Brian - Kens oldest friend whom he lost touch with years ago - proves quite the challenge. Elsewhere, Richards self-taped audition for a major role is troublesome and hes lost for words when Emily admits shes going on a date with a guy called Dave. Comedy, starring Gregor Fisher and Greg McHugh

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: While Richard goes to great lengths to hide the fact hes Norway-bound for his big TV break, Ken busies himself planning the special spot to scatter his beloved Jeans ashes - even if that means a less-than-sporting response from Rod at the football ground. As well as being a decoy for Richards departure, Emily is on hand to offer advice when things go awry for the Pritchards. Comedy, starring Greg McHugh, Gregor Fisher and Amy Lennnox

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: After being chatted up by Louises father, Jonny resolves to become more macho but things dont go as planned. Donna refuses to have a baby with Gaz, leaving him with only one option for getting the child he so desperately wants

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Part two of two. As Stacey continues to follow the emotional journey of the young women who have reported rape and now trying to navigate the justice system, she learns to appreciate that the statistics are stark. Fewer than two per cent of reported sexual assaults result in conviction, though this increases to over 50 per cent for cases that proceed to trial. As their trials near, the women learn that credibility also plays a pivotal role

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Invited into the traditionally private Gypsy community, Stacey discovers the complex balancing act young women face growing up in one of Britains most maligned ethnic minorities. In Bedfordshire she meets 23-year-old Chantelle, who prides herself in keeping with the old Gypsy values her grandmother taught her and shares them on social media, where she has nearly 400,000 TikTok followers. In Lincolnshire, the reporter meets 18-year-old Serena, who is planning her wedding to her fiance Marco. He is also from a Gypsy and Traveller community and Serena wouldnt have it any other way

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: In Britain shoplifting is at an all-time high, incidents of violence and abuse against shop staff have skyrocketed, and a third of Brits have admitted to stealing through the self-checkouts. To understand this apparent high-street epidemic, Stacey heads to Manchester to meet the people behind the crime and spends time on the shop floor with the staff trying to prevent further thefts

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Countless alien forces are drawn to a quiet backwater planet by a mysterious signal beaming out through time and space. The Doctor rescues Clara from a family Christmas dinner to help him discover what this message means, only to find that this insignificant planet has a terrible future, and that it falls to him to defend its peaceful inhabitants from the universes deadliest creatures. Matt Smith makes his final appearance as the Doctor in the 2013 Christmas special

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: As Mike and Alison head off to celebrate Christmas with Mikes parents, the ghosts are touched to discover that Alison has left a gift for each of them in the library, with strict instructions not to open them until the couple return. Touched by her generosity, the spectres are inspired to give her a special Christmas present in return - but with Alison due back in a matter of hours, can they pull off their ambitious plan in time?

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Christmas is just around the corner and Mikes mum Betty is staying - and testing Mike and Alisons patience with her interfering ways. Theres no easy way to ask her to go home so the couple - and the ghosts - struggle on, with Button House feeling fuller than ever. Now a convert, Robin is missing that all-important Christmassy feeling, so the gang members share their secrets to getting into the festive spirit - but when even more decorations and merry music dont do the trick, the ghosts wonder if Robin will be able to capture that Christmas magic again

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Socially awkward joke-shop owner Miranda is asked out to dinner by an old friend, prompting a visit to a clothing store to buy an outfit suitable for the date - but the expedition ends in disaster. First episode of the comedy, starring Miranda Hart, Sarah Hadland, Patricia Hodge and Sally Phillips. First aired in 2009

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: In a bid to encourage Gary to see her in a different light, Miranda joins an evening class to learn French, but is horrified to discover it is run by her former school teacher. The hapless singleton then decides a tango class is a more romantic option, only for her plans to take another turn for the worse. Comedy, guest starring Peter Davison, with Miranda Hart and Sarah Hadland

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Bit-part actor and only child Richard travels back from London to his family home in the Highlands to visit his elderly father Ken a year on from the passing of his mum. However, he soon realises his dad might be in need of a bit more full-time help, not that eccentric, strong-willed technophobe Ken would agree. Comedy, starring Gregor Fisher and Greg McHugh

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard decides the time has come to declutter his fathers house of all his junk, but Ken has suspicions over his sons true motives. The arrival of local handyman Mr Oddjob, Richards former school adversary Craig, complicates matters further as chaos commences. Comedy, starring Gregor Fisher and Greg McHugh

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Concerned that Ken is spending too much time on his own, Richard offers to take him to the local golf club, but his attempts to give his father a chance at being sociable are thwarted by officious clubhouse chairman Crawford. Before he knows it, Richard finds himself playing a much bigger part in integrating his dad back into the community than was originally intended

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Worried about his dads health, Richard battles officious receptionists - and a stubborn Ken - to get him a same-day appointment with Dr Roach. Later, Richard gets all-star struck when he learns his acting idol Lydia Bartlett is in Forres filming, while Emily suggests he imparts his wisdom to the local am-dram group. Guest starring Samantha Spiro

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Countless alien forces are drawn to a quiet backwater planet by a mysterious signal beaming out through time and space. Michelin-starred chefs Tom Kerridge and Lorna McNee and comedian Phil Wang join Chocolat author Joanne Harris on the judging panel. Theyre tasked with picking a winner from eight fantastic dessert courses

programme.desc: A look at the accusations of child sexual abuse made against Michael Jackson by a 13-year-old boy. Detectives describe the shock of investigating the worlds most famous performer, while inside Michaels camp, aides recall disbelief and a frantic effort to keep Michaels worldwide tour, business empire and public image intact

programme.desc: Dominic Chinea revisits stone carver Louise and stained-glass artist Charlotte to find out how they have built thriving businesses

programme.desc: Dion Dublin travels back to the 1970s in Nottinghamshire, and it all feels very familiar. Martin Roberts loves his surroundings in Bath, and Jacqui Joseph sees beyond the work that needs done in a Norfolk property to appreciate its potential

programme.desc: Clive Myrie meets distillery manager Luke in Bristol to explain how swapping his coffee and energy drinks for tea could lower his stress levels, reduce anxiety, calm the brain and support heart health

programme.desc: Michael Portillo leaves Kyushu by foot tunnel under the Kanmon straits to arrive on the main island of Honshu at the harbour town of Shimonoseki. Here, he encounters the deadly delicacy of pufferfish. Michael visits Hitachis Kasado Works, where the iconic Shinkansen high-speed trains are manufactured, before moving on to Hiroshima, where he meets Keiko Ogura, a bomb survivor who was just eight years old on that fateful day

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein lead the search with guest judge and passionate gardener Penny Lancaster to find Northern Irelands Greatest Garden. programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: George Anderson plants a bed of new raspberry varieties in his fruit cage and propagates new growth from Beechgroves existing fig tree, while Ruth Vichos returns to the vertical garden that she started last year. Theres a back to basics guide from Colin Crosbies plot just outside Dumfries, and a review of the gravel and grass garden, which will be thinned and replanted over the course of this season

programme.title: Great British Menu

programme.desc: A couple trying to get over the death of their daughter take a trip to Venice, where they are shocked by a psychics claim that the child is trying to contact them. Although the wife believes her story, the husband remains sceptical - until he begins to be troubled by spectral visions of his own. Nicholas Roegs supernatural thriller based on Daphne du Mauriers short story, starring Donald Sutherland and Julie Christie

programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.desc: Millie the Squid devises a set of tough games to test the Piris, and Pancake and Nugget must stick together and rely on their friendship to succeed

programme.desc: The ducklings look after the chipmunk when he accidentally gets hit during a game of baseball, so he pretends to be sick to get more and more privileges

programme.desc: The Lemmings use cans and tins to build a beach slide, which annoys Grizzy.

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary following a research project at Edinburgh Zoo aimed at providing chimpanzees with the training necessary to shoot a film. Chimp specialist Betsy Herrelko tries to communicate with the primates using video technology, as they are given cameras and taught how to use them. The filmed results can be seen at the end of the programme. Stuart McQuarrie narrates

programme.title: Live Womens Six Nations
programme.desc: England v Wales (Kick-off 2.15pm). Coverage of the opening match in the third round of fixtures, held at Ashton Gate in Bristol. England claimed an emphatic 67-12 victory in Wales last year and went on to win another Grand Slam, while the Welsh finished at the bottom of the table, and the hosts also won here 46-10 when the sides met in the 2024 competition

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the results and this afternoons news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Longleat in Wiltshire, where expert Claire Rawle spots an unusual Tibetan teapot among the offerings brought in for valuation and it leads to the discovery of similar artefacts. The goods all go under the hammer and with worldwide interest on the objects, there is the potential for a record-breaking sale. Meanwhile, Paul explores Longleat House to find out more about its Elizabethan architecture and controversial creator

programme.title: Murder on the Orient Express (1974)
programme.desc: An American businessman travelling on the Orient Express from Istanbul to Paris tries to hire Hercule Poirot to discover the source of the death threats that have been made against him. The sleuth refuses, but wakes up the following morning to learn the man has been stabbed to death while the train was held up by heavy snow - and there is no shortage of suspects among the passengers. Agatha Christie mystery, starring Albert Finney, Lauren Bacall, Ingrid Bergman, Sean Connery and Vanessa Redgrave

programme.title: Live Womens Six Nations
programme.desc: France v Ireland (kick-off 8.10pm). All the action from the concluding match in the third round of fixtures, held at Stade Marcel-Michelin. France have won the last eight meetings between the sides, including a 27-15 victory at Affidea Stadium last year and a 38-17 triumph in the last contest on French soil in 2024. Ireland also lost their first match on their travels in this seasons competition, losing out 33-12 to holders England, while their opponents this evening got off to a winning start with a 40-7 home win at Stade des Alpes

programme.title: Black British Music at the BBC: Volume 2
programme.desc: DJ Spoony presents a personal selection of archive performances in a celebration of some of the very best in black British music from over the decades. The playlist features 40 tracks from the worlds of pop, soul, ska, reggae, rap and garage, with artists including the likes of Seal, the Selecter, Soul II Soul, FKA Twigs, Miss Dynamite and Shirley Bassey.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra

She finally thinks her luck has changed, but when she discovers who the man of her dreams is, she cannot be sure how her parents will react. Romantic crime drama, starring Emma Laird and Loyle Carner

programme.title: McCartney: The Hunt for the Lost Bass
programme.desc: The disappearance of Paul McCartneys original Hofner bass more than 50 years ago is one of rock n rolls great mysteries and this documentary tells the story of the instrument and the quest to find it. Featuring interviews with the former Beatle, his brother Mike, musicians Klaus Voormann and Elvis Costello, and roadies, journalists and fans who embarked on a mission to return the bass to its place in music history

programme.desc: At storage auctions across Canada, high-stakes buyers convene daily. Here, Roy suits up in full army gear to declare war on the buyers in Scarborough

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: Roy comes to the auction in Scarborough bearing gifts, but no one is impressed. Rick tries his best to turn Cindys frown upside-down

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team of antiques dealers visit Barnsley in South Yorkshire, where Tim Hogarth assesses a collection of vinyl records and Helen Gardiner is pleased to appraise a Lorenzl figurine

programme.title: WWEs Greatest Moments
programme.desc: Unforgettable moments featuring WWE superstars, highlighting the tag teams of the 1990s

programme.title: WWEs Greatest Moments
programme.desc: Unforgettable moments featuring WWE superstars, with action covering the 1980s onwards

programme.title: The UnBelievable with Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: From a million bats armed with tiny bombs to inflatable tanks, robot assassins, bazooka Vespas, and even war dolphins, the host delves into the eccentric and sometimes downright strange innovations that have graced the battlefield. This captivating exploration pushes the boundaries of what was once deemed possible, showcasing the ingenuity and, at times, the absurdity that has characterised the history of military technology

programme.title: Paranormal: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Documentary the most chilling and unexplainable paranormal activity footage captured by eyewitnesses

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Experts investigate footage of a van hurtling out of control across a roundabout, narrowly avoiding pedestrians, before smashing into a car. A concentrated jet of fire shot out its side, sending a ribbon of flame dozens of feet across the street

programme.title: UFO POV
programme.desc: Eye-witness accounts and footage, plus expert analysis and innovative special effects show what UFOs might see from their own perspective

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Now, in the 21st century, the search for extraterrestrial intelligence is ramping up like never before, with powerful new technologies allowing the exploration of billions of stars across the Universe. How far away is First Contact?

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Cameras in Texas capture a 42-foot spiders web capable of capturing a human. A Baltimore street collapses into a mysterious void which some experts believe are the remnants of legendary tunnels built during the War of 1812

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Seth intervenes in a confrontation between a customer and an employee in the car park, and is later given a 30th birthday surprise by his colleagues

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: A family feud leads to Ashleys resignation. Meanwhile, a couple of women try to sell a dirty fur coat and a man offers to pawn his gold tooth

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les misses Ashleys presence in the shop, while a man employs menacing tactics to persuade the dealers to buy a couple of outdated computer monitors

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: Host David Dickinson and antique dealers Simon Scheider, Mark Stevens, Michael Hobden and Jan Keyne visit Loughborough in Leicestershire, where members of the public bring in Corgi and Dinky toys for their inspection and Mark encounters a stunning diamond ring

programme.title: Wicked Tuna
programme.desc: The captains face major storm fronts, and are forced to choose between braving the elements or remaining in port until the conditions have cleared up

programme.title: Wicked Tuna
programme.desc: The crews enter the final two weeks of the season, and face a daily struggle to retain their leads or avoid ending up at the bottom of the table

programme.title: Swamp People: Serpent Invasion
programme.desc: Troy and Pickle witness how far the pythons have spread as Zak and Aaron take on a new type of beast. Meanwhile, Bill and Tes tackle some uninvited guests

programme.title: Swamp People: Serpent Invasion
programme.desc: Troy and Pickle face a familiar foe, while Bill and Tes defend a friends camp from a slithering invasion. Zak and Aarons hunt leaves a bad taste in their mouths

programme.title: Gold Wars Downunder
programme.desc: Three hardcore mining teams battle it out on the one patch of gold-rich-land at the town of Clermont to see who can unearth the biggest nuggets and the most gold. There are nine weeks left of the gold season to go and the pressure is on. At Swiss Diggings, an unemployed Donnie begs owner Dennis for a new job

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The mysteries of the Aztecs, who built one of the worlds most powerful and feared empires. They claimed that they were guided by a pantheon of more than a thousand gods who came to Earth from different parts of the Universe

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: UFO Over the Andes, the Ohio Grassman and Ancient Horn Mystery
programme.desc: The legendary Ohio Grassman is apparently captured in footage of a tall, dark-coloured, hairy creature walking through woods. Plus, a look at the Chilean family photograph of a real UFO flying above the Andes Mountains and the notion that a 5,000-year-old horn discovered on a beach in England belongs to a prehistoric creature that still lives

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Men in Black Calls, Old Kentucky Aliens and Mysterious Eclipse
programme.desc: Tony Harris and his team of experts examine several seemingly impossible events, such as a family managing to survive a bolt of lightning ripping through their car during a Kansas storm, a bizarre creature spotted on a driveway camera and an image captured of an alien probe

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Documentary series focusing on six unfathomable mysteries - from a gigantic tsunami that never hits land, to a sinister blood-red lake in war-torn Baghdad

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: The series telling the stories behind remarkable satellite images continues, focusing on strange barren rings in the Namib Desert and a lost city in the heart of the Amazon

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Photographs of a 25-mile structure known as the eye of the Sahara, and an extraordinary cloud emanating from an island in the Indian Ocean

programme.title: Cursed Histories
programme.desc: More instances of the granting of so-called curses. This episode highlights the Egyptian royals who promised turmoil for anyone who dares disturb their eternal rest. Plus, a devilish figure at the Mississippi crossroads who granted great musical talents in exchange for a bluesmans soul and the pantheon of gods with the apparent power to punish any accursed name carved into dark tablets of ancient lead

programme.title: Cursed Histories
programme.desc: Elements, places, icons or events that seem to be plagued by what can only be described as a curse. This episode focuses on a damned amethyst gem, a devilish beast and the downfall of a family for several generations.

programme.title: Secret Societies: In the Shadows
programme.desc: The murky history of secret societies, examining heir origins and their intent, assessing whether they intended to pull the levers of power or aim to destroy those in charge. The Knights Templar owned vast swathes of land, commanded fleets, and lent money to kings. This documentary seeks to separate the facts from the fiction

programme.title: Holy Marvels with Dennis Quaid
programme.desc: The Hollywood actor considers buildings of Biblical proportions. From a grand basilica in Vatican City and the towering monuments of Ancient Egypt to a Biblical tower and a lost city in the clouds of Peru, he uncovers their construction, purpose and the enduring mysteries they hold

programme.title: Field of Vampires
programme.desc: A plot in Poland containing 30 deviant burials. One woman was buried with a sickle across her neck and a padlock on her toe - this corpse was feared to be a real-life vampire. An international team of experts uses cutting-edge science and historical research to investigate who this woman was and why she was so feared

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: How mourners in Central Asia were shocked to enter their village graveyard and find row upon row of holes. The graves were all empty - but where had the bodies gone? Plus, a car on a busy Moscow street that seemed to collide with an invisible object

programme.title: Paranormal: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Documentary the most chilling and unexplainable paranormal activity footage captured by eyewitnesses

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: A look at Cleveland National Forest, where reports of gun-wielding ghosts, savage creatures and otherworldly portals are rife

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: A trip through the ginormous forest in Arizona. Strange encounters rumoured to have happened there include an alien abduction

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: Alien encounters have offered a possible glimpse into the secrets of interstellar navigation. Giorgio Tsoukalos contemplates whether this might one day ensure mankinds survival

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: Giorgio Tsoukalos reveals how advances in technology have given unprecedented access to distant galaxies that may harbour life. However, alien life may be found closer to home

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: As the world moves into the digital age, more people are capturing images in the sky that they are unable to explain, leading to an influx of reported accounts of alien encounters

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: For years many people have argued that UFOs are terrestrial in reality. Though they may be correct, it is unlikely to be in the way that they think they are

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: The year 1947 saw an unprecedented wave of UFO sightings that propelled the phenomenon to the world stage. Giorgio Tsoukalos examines the US Air Forces findings

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: For many people, the most credible UFO reports are those raised by police officers. This programme investigates the most significant reports from police records

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: Between 1981 and 2011, cameras on Nasa space shuttles have picked up many images of UFOs that experts still struggle to explain
